idea-plugin.name: Codebase Notes
idea-plugin.description:
Shared, Git-versioned notes for your codebase structure — across JetBrains IDEs, VS Code, and TRAE.

Put explanations such as “why this directory exists”, “where the project core is”, and “where new features plug in” directly beside files and directories in the Project view — instead of losing them in chat history, personal notes, or outdated documentation.

Codebase Notes stores all notes in a repository-local .codebase-notes.json file. Commit it to Git so teammates and users of other supported editors can share the same context.

What it helps with

  • Onboarding: mark the core order flow or the main application entry.
  • Architecture: identify public API boundaries and important modules.
  • Maintenance: explain generated files and paths that should not be edited manually.
  • Navigation: mark stable sources of truth and extension points.

Core features

  • Edit, style, or remove notes from the Project view context menu.
  • Display short notes directly beside files and directories.
  • Use six semantic colors to turn the project tree into a code map.
  • Keep note paths in sync when files or directories are renamed or moved.
  • Share one open JSON format with VS Code and TRAE.
  • English and Simplified Chinese UI that follows the IDE language.
  • No account, cloud service, telemetry, or proprietary database.

Six code-map styles

  • Core (red): the project backbone — learn this first.
  • Focus (yellow): easy-to-miss relationships, constraints, or details.
  • Important (blue): important modules and engineering entry points.
  • Stable (green): trusted sources of truth and reliable baselines.
  • Extension (purple): places where new capabilities plug in.
  • Default (gray): ordinary responsibility descriptions.

Colors describe code structure rather than errors or runtime status: red for the backbone, yellow for details, blue for importance, green for trusted baselines, and purple for extension points.

Getting started

  1. Open a project in a supported JetBrains IDE.
  2. Right-click a file or directory in the Project view.
  3. Choose Codebase Notes → Edit Text Note.
  4. Commit .codebase-notes.json to Git if the note should be shared.

Default shortcuts: Option+R on macOS or Alt+R on Windows/Linux to edit text; Shift+Option+R or Shift+Alt+R to set a style.

Data and privacy

  • Notes stay inside the opened project.
  • Writes use locking, temporary files, and conflict detection to avoid silent overwrites.
  • The JSON format is validated by a shared schema and cross-language conformance tests.
  • The plugin does not collect or transmit project data.

Source code and documentation · Report an issue or request a feature · Latest release
idea-plugin.change-notes:
0.1.24

  • Unified JetBrains, VS Code, and TRAE around six code-map styles: Default, Important, Focus, Core, Stable, and Extension.
  • Added a shared color model for identifying the project backbone, details, important modules, trusted baselines, and extension points.
  • Added an AI initialization guide for generating concise project notes with consistent semantics.
  • Removed a Kotlin-generated deprecated API compatibility bridge reported by Marketplace validation.

Full release history
